_docs.purpose: Config-front-end mapping contract: each case maps one zzop.config.jsonc object to the exact facade request JSON the PURE mapper must emit. Pinned by crates/config/src/mapper.rs via crates/config/tests/config_parity.rs (deep-equal after reversing its two documented deltas, listed under normalization). Historically this was a two-sided JS<->Rust parity fixture against the npm JS CLI's mapper.js; the JS implementation was removed (2026-07-20, then its 2026-07-23 npm revival ships the native binary with no JS mapper), so the expected shapes remain in the RAW form that side emitted — see normalization. Same committed-fixture pattern as docs/adapters/key-normalization.fixture.json.
normalization: Path resolution (the ONE documented behavioral deviation, crates/config/src/lib.rs): the historical JS CLI left root/cacheDir/packsDir as literal strings for the process cwd to resolve; the Rust mapper resolves them against the config file's directory. Expected values below are the RAW (JS-era) form; the Rust test maps its resolved absolute paths back to this form against its known base directory (base itself -> ".") before comparing, so any OTHER path difference still fails.

withDefaults fold-in (a packaging difference, not a behavioral one, crates/config/src/mapper.rs module doc): the Rust mapper folds the historical JS loader's withDefaults layer in — bundled packs as inline packDefs on every tree, plus git: {} when the config has no git key. The expected shapes below carry neither (the JS side applied withDefaults later, as a bundled packsDir prepend). The Rust test asserts both injections happened (one packDefs entry per BUNDLED_PACK_SOURCES pack; git exactly {}) and strips them before comparing.

Object key order is not part of the contract (serde_json BTreeMap vs JS insertion order); both tests compare as parsed JSON values, which is key-order-insensitive for objects and order-SENSITIVE for arrays. Fixture rule ids are written in alphabetical order so derived array order (disabledRules, suppressions) is identical on both sides by construction.
_docs.scope: The pure config->request mapping only. Discovery (zzop.config.jsonc lookup), JSONC stripping, trees:"auto" expansion, overlay file reads (filesystem-dependent, warnings-channel), and warning texts are covered by each side's own tests, not this fixture.

cases.name: empty config maps to a single-tree analyze of the current root
packs extraDirs and disabled map to packsDir and disabledRules
git knobs, cacheDir and sizeCap pass through
multi-tree with sourceIds, mountedAt, mounts and hosts (sourceId defaults to the raw root)
rule excludes split glob vs substring; top-level exclude maps to globalExcludes
severity aliases normalize; off routes to disabledRules
multiple roots map to analyzeTrees with each raw root as its sourceId
